name: Chainguard Images & Registry API
what_it_does: Software supply chain security platform providing minimal, zero-CVE container base images and private container registry with REST API. Images are signed with Sigstore and rebuilt daily to patch vulnerabilities.
use_cases: Pulling zero-CVE base images for containerized applications to reduce vulnerability surface
Querying Chainguard Images catalog to find minimal base images for specific languages/runtimes
Managing private image repositories and access controls for enterprise image distribution
Verifying image provenance and Sigstore attestations programmatically
Automating compliance checks by querying SBOM attestations for deployed images
not_for: General-purpose container registry without supply chain security requirements
Teams comfortable with larger image sizes and periodic patching
best_when: An agent enforces supply chain security by pulling from verified, minimal, zero-CVE base images with Sigstore provenance for containerized deployments.
avoid_when: Image size and build time are less important than ecosystem compatibility — standard Docker Hub images are simpler.

auth.notes: OIDC-based authentication via chainctl CLI or API tokens. Private registry access uses token-based auth. Public images available without auth. Enterprise features require Chainguard account.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer images: free for public/personal use; limited selection
pricing.paid_tiers: Production images: from $5k-$50k+/year depending on image count and support
pricing.notes: Developer tier free with limited image catalog. Production tier adds daily rebuilds guarantee, SLA, and extended support. Enterprise adds private images and compliance features.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation covers image catalog and chainctl CLI. REST API for registry follows OCI distribution spec. SBOM and Sigstore attestation endpoints documented. API surface is primarily for image management.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Image pull and verification operations are inherently idempotent. Registry operations follow OCI spec idempotency guarantees.
known_agent_gotchas: chainctl CLI is the primary management interface — REST API is supplementary
OIDC-based auth requires identity provider setup — not just API key
Image tags are mutable but digests are immutable — use digest-pinned references for reproducibility
Wolfi-based images may lack packages found in Debian/Alpine — dependency compatibility check needed

security_score_components.security_notes: Founded by ex-Google supply chain security engineers. Sigstore signing for all images. OIDC-based authentication. Images built on Wolfi — minimal footprint, zero known CVEs. SOC2 certified. Gold standard for supply chain security.
